claim_text: Vision Transformer applies a pure transformer directly to sequences of image patches for classification.
label: GROUNDED
notes: straight paraphrase — safe positive baseline

claim_text: With large-scale pretraining, ViT can match state-of-the-art CNNs while using less compute to train.
label: GROUNDED
notes: paraphrases the transfer + compute claim

claim_text: Vision Transformer achieves 96.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet without any pretraining.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract makes no such accuracy claim; also contradicts the pretraining requirement

claim_text: ViT outperforms all CNN architectures across every image recognition benchmark tested to date.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract only claims 'excellent results compared to SOTA' on the tested benchmarks — not 'all CNN architectures on every benchmark'

claim_text: The Vision Transformer paper introduced the concept of self-attention.
label: FABRICATED
notes: self-attention predates ViT (Vaswani 2017); paper only applies transformers to vision

claim_text: RAG combines a pre-trained parametric seq2seq model with a non-parametric dense vector index of Wikipedia.
label: GROUNDED
notes: direct paraphrase of the method

claim_text: RAG sets a new state of the art on three open-domain question answering tasks.
label: GROUNDED
notes: minor paraphrase of a specific quantitative claim

claim_text: Using RAG causes the model's factual accuracy to improve by exactly 12% on all NLP tasks.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract has no such number; also 'causes' overstates

claim_text: The RAG paper shows retrieval-augmented models beat GPT-4 on open-domain QA.
label: FABRICATED
notes: GPT-4 comparison never appears in this 2020 abstract

claim_text: Because RAG uses a differentiable retriever, it eliminates hallucinations in language generation.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract discusses provenance and updates as 'open problems', not solved; no elimination claim

claim_text: Fisher Vectors show natural resilience to rotations while CNNs lack a built-in mechanism for rotation invariance.
label: GROUNDED
notes: direct paraphrase of rotation-robustness finding

claim_text: Neither CNN features nor Fisher Vectors consistently outperform the other across benchmarks, and combining them typically yields the best retrieval results.
label: GROUNDED
notes: restates 'no systematic winner + combining helps' in different words

claim_text: Fisher Vectors outperform CNNs by 12% on rotated images across all benchmarks tested
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract makes no numeric claim about rotation — invented 12%

claim_text: CNN-based descriptors are strictly better than Fisher Vectors for all image retrieval tasks.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract explicitly says no descriptor is systematically better

claim_text: Fisher Vectors outperform the ViT-based retrieval baseline on standard benchmarks
label: FABRICATED
notes: 2015 paper never compares to ViT (2020)

claim_text: PSRD reduces the hallucination rate of LLaVA-1.5-7B by 50.0% and outperforms existing post-hoc methods across five hallucination benchmarks
label: GROUNDED
notes: direct paraphrase of the headline numeric result

claim_text: Vision hallucinations in LVLMs follow a temporal pattern where they spike at the onset of each new semantic phase during generation.
label: GROUNDED
notes: restates the phase-wise dynamic-pattern finding using different phrasing

claim_text: PSRD reduces the hallucination rate of LLaVA-1.5-7B by 65% across evaluation benchmarks
label: FABRICATED
notes: changed 50.0% → 65% while keeping the rest identical

claim_text: PSRD consistently outperforms all existing hallucination mitigation methods across every publicly available LVLM benchmark
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract says 'existing post-hoc methods across five benchmarks for four LVLMs' — 'all methods on every benchmark' is a strict superset

claim_text: The PSRD framework reduces hallucination by fine-tuning LLaVA-1.5-7B on large-scale annotated data.
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract explicitly says PSRD works at inference time 'without external supervision' — this attributes a different method (fine-tuning) to it

claim_text: On a controlled financial QA dataset with GPT-3.5-turbo, ECLIPSE achieves ROC AUC of 0.89 and average precision of 0.90
label: GROUNDED
notes: direct paraphrase of the headline evaluation result

claim_text: ECLIPSE substantially outperforms a semantic-entropy-only baseline, which achieves ROC AUC of only 0.50 on the same financial QA benchmark
label: GROUNDED
notes: restates the baseline comparison using different word order

claim_text: On the financial QA benchmark, ECLIPSE achieves ROC AUC of 0.94 and average precision of 0.95
label: FABRICATED
notes: bumped 0.89 → 0.94 and 0.90 → 0.95 while keeping framing identical

claim_text: Because ECLIPSE is logprob-native, it will fail on any model that does not expose token-level log probabilities
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract shows Claude-3-Haiku ablation dropped to AUC 0.59 (still above chance, not zero) — 'will fail on any' is stronger than the observed degradation

claim_text: ECLIPSE outperforms retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) baselines on the financial QA benchmark
label: FABRICATED
notes: abstract never compares ECLIPSE to RAG — the only baseline mentioned is semantic-entropy-only
